symptoms.symptom_name: High-frequency non-bloody watery diarrhoea with cramps

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secretory non-bloody watery diarrhoea is characteristic of microscopic colitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal urine output, clear mentation, mild postural dizziness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved urine output and clear mentation indicate dehydration is currently mild.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blood/black stool, fever, antibiotics or travel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever/blood/antibiotic/travel history lowers infective/ischaemic probability.
Known microscopic colitis supports a flare as the cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day face-to-face review with observations if dehydration worsens
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess postural BP/hydration and consider IV fluids if oral rehydration fails.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool testing (C. difficile, culture) and electrolytes if persistent
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude infection and assess electrolytes if the flare continues.
